turn1_code:
def format_blog_title(title: str) -> str:
    """Formats the title of a blog post for display in markdown.

    Args:
        title: The title of the blog post.

    Returns:
        A string formatted as '# {title}'.
    """
    return f"# {title}"
turn2_code:
def format_blog_post(title: str, content: str) -> str:
    """Formats the blog post title and content for display in markdown.

    Args:
        title: The title of the blog post.
        content: The content of the blog post.

    Returns:
        A string formatted as '# {title}\n{content}'.
    """
    return f"# {title}\n{content}"

BLOG_FORMAT = "

{subject}

{description}

" def publish_blog_article(subject: str, description: str) -> str: """ Sanitizes and formats a user-submitted blog post for safe web display. The function takes a subject (title) and a description (content) for the blog post. Both inputs are first sanitized using `html.escape` to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerabilities by escaping special HTML characters. The sanitized inputs are then formatted into an HTML string using a predefined template (`BLOG_FORMAT`). Args: subject: The heading (title) of the blog post. This will be placed within

tags. description: The main text content (body) of the blog post. This will be placed within

tags. Returns: A string containing the HTML-formatted and sanitized blog post, ready for safe display on a webpage. """ # Sanitize the subject and description to prevent XSS attacks. # html.escape replaces special characters (e.g., <, >, &) # with their corresponding HTML entities (e.g., <, >, &). sanitized_subject = html.escape(subject) sanitized_description = html.escape(description) # Format the sanitized content using the predefined blog template. # The .format() method substitutes the placeholders in BLOG_FORMAT # with the sanitized subject and description. formatted_post = BLOG_FORMAT.format(subject=sanitized_subject, description=sanitized_description) # Log the successful formatting of the blog post. logger.info(f"Blog post with subject '{subject}' successfully formatted and ready for display.") return formatted_post
